title: crupina intermedia
intro: Crupina intermedia, also known as Yellow Starthistle, is a thistle-like annual or biennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and sharp, prickly leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic effects. The health benefits associated with Crupina intermedia include its ability to treat various conditions such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and menstrual disorders. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
